slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
Meridian Holdings' Information Security Policy v3.2 represents a significant evolution from v3.1, addressing critical gaps in incident response timeliness and vendor oversight while introducing stronger preventive controls and extended log retention. The update shifts the organization from a reactive to a proactive security posture, directly improving regulatory compliance readiness—particularly under GDPR and CCPA—by mandating 24-hour incident notification for customer-data breaches and extending log retention to 12 months. However, these enhancements come with immediate operational demands, requiring IT and security teams to revalidate privileged access and close vendor evidence gaps within a 30-day transition window, creating a short-term workload burden.

- v3.2 mandates MFA for all workforce accounts, whereas v3.1 only established foundational MFA controls without universal enforcement.  
- v3.2 introduces quarterly privileged access revalidation and a 30-day transition requirement for managers to revalidate access, absent in v3.1.  
- v3.2 requires pre-onboarding and periodic reassessments for critical vendors, strengthening vendor risk management beyond v3.1’s annual evidence requirements.  
- v3.2 tightens incident escalation to 24 hours for customer-data breaches, resolving v3.1’s critical gap in undefined notification timelines.  
- v3.2 extends log retention to 12 months in tamper-evident storage, up from v3.1’s 180-day baseline, enhancing forensic and audit readiness.  
- The 30-day transition period in v3.2 creates urgent operational workload for IT and security teams, a temporary but significant implementation burden not present in v3.1.  

Recommendation: Prioritize execution of the 30-day transition tasks to revalidate privileged access and remediate vendor documentation, as this enables immediate realization of v3.2’s improved compliance and risk reduction benefits; monitor team capacity during this window to avoid burnout or oversight.
